title: Echinocoryne echinocephala
intro: Echinocoryne echinocephala, commonly known as the Hedgehog Flower, is a medicinal plant belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its therapeutic properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant benefits, making it useful for treating various health conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Echinocoryne echinocephala include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly consumed for their health benefits.
taxonomy: Echinocoryne echinocephala is classified as a type of flowering plant, specifically within the family Acanthaceae. It belongs to the order Lamiales and is a member of the genus Echinocoryne. This plant is native to tropical Africa and is characterized by its unique appearance and growth habits. Its classification is further supported by its morphology and molecular characteristics. At the species level, Echinocoryne echinocephala is a unique and distinct species within the Echinocoryne genus.
